name: PubMatic Programmatic Ad Platform API
what_it_does: Publisher-focused supply-side platform (SSP) with REST API — providing header bidding infrastructure, ad quality management, private marketplace (PMP) deal management, connected TV (CTV) monetization, and audience data activation for digital publishers to maximize ad revenue across all channels and formats.
use_cases: Managing header bidding configuration and demand partner connections via PubMatic API
Querying publisher ad revenue and fill rate data for monetization analytics reporting
Configuring private marketplace (PMP) deals with premium advertisers via API
Managing ad quality rules and brand safety settings programmatically
Accessing CTV inventory monetization and reporting for streaming publishers
not_for: Advertiser-side campaign management (PubMatic is publisher-focused SSP — use DSPs for buying)
Small publishers (<1M monthly impressions) — SSP value requires meaningful inventory scale
Social media advertising (PubMatic accesses open internet, not walled gardens)
best_when: A digital publisher (web, app, CTV) needs an independent SSP partner to maximize programmatic revenue via header bidding, private marketplace deals, and unified auction — without the conflict of interest of a Google-owned SSP.
avoid_when: You're an advertiser (not a publisher), have low traffic volume, or primarily monetize through direct sales without programmatic demand.

auth.notes: API key authentication. HTTP Basic auth for legacy endpoints. Account-scoped access. No webhooks — must poll for reporting. OpenWrap (header bidding solution) configured via dashboard and API. PMP deal management via Deal API. CTV monetization via separate CTV API. No OAuth2. Publisher API separate from marketplace API.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; revenue share model
pricing.paid_tiers: Revenue share on monetized impressions; contact sales
pricing.notes: San Jose company. Founded 2006. NASDAQ listed (PUBM). $280M+ revenue (2023). 1,600+ publisher customers including CBS Interactive, USA Today, Tribune Publishing. OpenWrap header bidding container is key product — open source (PrebidJS-based). Independent SSP (not owned by DSP) is key differentiator vs Google's DFP/AdX. CTV and streaming monetization growing. Identity Hub for post-cookie identity resolution.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API for inventory, deals, reporting, and ad quality. API key and Basic auth. Moderate public documentation — enterprise accounts get dedicated support. OpenWrap header bidding requires JavaScript implementation on publisher site. PMP deal configuration via Deal API. No webhooks — polling required for reporting data.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Reporting reads fully idempotent. Deal reads idempotent. Inventory configuration updates may not be idempotent — use ID for updates.
known_agent_gotchas: TCF2 (Transparency and Consent Framework) implementation required for EU/GDPR compliance
OpenWrap header bidding requires JavaScript on publisher site — server-side HB available but limited
PMP deals require buyer approval — deal setup is multi-party process
CTV monetization separate API — different endpoints from web display monetization
Revenue share model — payment terms and reconciliation require careful accounting setup

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. GDPR, CCPA, TCF2 compliant. US, EU, IN data residency. API key auth. NASDAQ-listed with public company security standards. Publisher revenue data is highly sensitive — restrict API access strictly. TCF2 consent management required for EU impression monetization. Ad quality management requires continuous monitoring for malvertising.
